Job Title: Grants and Communications Officer – Engagement Team
Job Description:
Location
: Hanoi, Vietnam
Effective date:
June 2026
Reports to

Employment Type
: Full-time
ABOUT ACTION ON POVERTY
Action on Poverty is a
venture philanthropy accelerator
focused on turning locally led solutions into
scalable, evidence
‑
based answers to global poverty
.
We exist because poverty persists not due to a lack of potential, but because proven solutions often lack the capital, connections and systems needed to scale. Our role is to identify what works, strengthen it, and help it grow to reach millions of people permanently.
For almost 60 years, we’ve partnered with entrepreneurs, communities and funders across
Asia, Africa and the Pacific
, supporting solutions that become embedded in markets, institutions or public policy rather than remaining reliant on ongoing aid.
The Engagement Team plays a critical role in how we communicate this work, build relationships with partners and supporters, and position Action on Poverty as a credible and strategic partner in international development and philanthropy.
ROLE PURPOSE
The Grants & Communications Assistant provides practical, hands-on support across
communications, digital content, and grant writing
.
Reporting to the Engagement team in Australia, this role is suited to an early-career professional who is interested in the intersection of
communications, philanthropy and international development
, and wants to build capability in both
content and institutional funding
.
You will support the delivery of communications and engagement activities, while playing an active role in the
development of funding proposals and donor-facing materials
that support the growth of Action on Poverty’s solution portfolio.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Communications & Content
Draft clear, concise written content for digital channels, donor communications, newsletters and internal use
Produce and edit short-form content for social media, including basic video editing
Manage and schedule content across platforms, with a focus on LinkedIn
Design digital assets using Canva in line with Action on Poverty’s brand
Support thought leadership and external communications, including research, drafting and content preparation
Prospect Research, Proposals & Fundraising Support
Identify relevant grant opportunities aligned with Action on Poverty’s priorities
Maintain the grants calendar, tracking deadlines, submissions and reporting requirements
Draft and contribute to grant applications, concept notes and proposals
Translate program information and data into clear, structured funding narratives
Review funding guidelines to ensure proposals are accurate and compliant
Prepare donor reports and grant-related communications, coordinating inputs from program, finance and leadership teams
Maintain accurate records of submissions and grant documentation
Events & Engagement
Support the coordination and delivery of events, both in-person and online
Assist with event communications, invitations, run sheets and follow-up activity
Contribute to donor and partner engagement activities as required
Administration & Team Support
Manage and triage shared grants, engagement and communications inboxes
Support calendar management and meeting coordination
Provide general administrative support to ensure smooth day-to-day team operations
Monitor news and updates across the Australian philanthropy landscape
ABOUT YOU
This role would suit a graduate or early‑career professional with:
A degree in Communications, International Relations, or a related field
A strong interest in international development, philanthropy and systems‑change approaches to poverty
Curiosity about how philanthropic funding decisions are made, and interest in prospect research and proposal development
A demonstrated interest in
grant writing, proposal development and donor communications
A proactive and detail‑oriented approach
Skills & Experience
Essential
Excellent written English skills
, with the ability to structure clear and compelling narratives
Strong attention to detail and editing capability
Confidence using social media, particularly
LinkedIn
High competence in
Canva
Strong organisational skills and ability to manage multiple deadlines
Ability to work independently while taking direction
Desirable
Experience supporting or contributing to
grant writing or proposal development
Understanding of funding or grant application processes
Experience working with a
CRM
(HubSpot preferred)
Basic videography and content production skills
Exposure to PR or earned media
Experience supporting events or campaigns
Experience working in a cross-border or international context
You are passionate, driven and eager to learn. You can juggle multiple tasks, take initiative, and contribute thoughtfully to a small, collaborative team and have excellent attention to detail. You’re looking to build a meaningful career in engagement within international development.
WHAT IS IN IT FOR YOU?
Meaningful work contributing to poverty reduction and community empowerment.
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements.
Additional annual leave, sick leave, wellbeing and birthday leave.
Supportive, inclusive, and collaborative team culture.
Professional learning and development opportunities.
Cross-cultural working environment with global collaboration opportunities.
Employee wellbeing initiatives and engagement activities.
Competitive benefits package in accordance with AOP policies.
At Action on Poverty, we empower our people to thrive. We offer a purpose-driven career, flexibility, and a supportive culture that values wellbeing, diversity, and growth.

Job Title: [Extension] Project Administrator, Vinh Long
Job Description:
Samaritan’s Purse is a Christian humanitarian organization providing aid to hurting people around the world. With a sustainable history working in Vietnam, the current projects include health, education, relief, protection, water, sanitation, and hygiene projects in schools, and livelihood projects.
Samaritan’s Purse Vietnam’s projects are being implemented in Ha Noi, Lai Chau, Lao Cai, Da Nang, Ho Chi Minh and Vinh Long, where the majority of people are from ethnic minority groups, often the most vulnerable or marginalized sections of the population.
We are now seeking highly qualified candidate for the following position:
Position Title:
Project Administrator, Vinh Long
Grade:
Administrator
Position Duration:
From hiring date to 31/12/2026
Duty Station:
HCMC with frequent travel to project locations
Reports To:
Lead Project Coordinator, Vinh Long
Job Summary:
The Project Administrator supports the program of Samaritan’s Purse under the direction of the Lead Project Coordinator. He/she is responsible to support project activities including initiating, planning, execution, monitoring/evaluation, and conclusion of activities.
Key Responsibilities:
Project Implementation:
Support Program Manager and Lead Project Coordinator/Project Coordinator(s) with project design and implementation support including conducting activities, coordination, trainings, assessments, monitoring, and evaluation.
Logistical Arrangements:
Prepare logistical arrangements including transportation, accommodations, meals, and supplies for project activities and distributions.
Training:
Assist with training, development of supporting materials, and reviewing partner’s training for accuracy and applicability.
Scheduling:
Manage timely completion of the project activities under the direction of the Program Manager in coordination with partners.
Procurement:
Utilize established office procedures to ensure the project has the necessary products and/or services to complete work in a timely manner.  File and organize procurement documents in the shared drive. Keep inventory of project supplies in storage.
Monitoring and Evaluation:
Monitor project activities conducted by Samaritan’s Purse and partners. Utilize survey, interview, discussion, and other data collection methods to monitor and evaluate project activities. Ensure activity and project outputs meet defined scope and quality.
Reporting and Data Entry:
Data is compiled from surveys, questionnaires, and activities to create summary of information for input into reports including data software, monthly, and annual reports for review by the Program
Stakeholder, Partner, and Government Relations:
Assist the Lead Project Coordinator with meetings, communications, and related documentation. Works to foster strong relationships with partnering authorities.
Financial
M
anagement:
Follow internal financial procedures to ensure timely completion of activities in accordance with approved budgets and donor requirements. Collect accurate receipts and supporting financial documents. Create regular financial expense reports for activities. Review partner financial reporting for compliance with internal requirements when needed.
Quality:
Oversee activities to ensure they meet the quality needs for which it was approved.
Communication:
Ensure timely and appropriate planning, collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, control, monitoring of project communication, including impact stories.
Work Environment:
Nurture a positive work environment and foster a commitment to Samaritan’s Purse values and ethics.
Other responsibilities:
Samaritan’s Purse works as a team to carry out programming and additional responsibilities will be assigned as needed.
Education/Experience Needed:
Bachelor’s Degree in development-related field, social sciences, and/or other relevant field.
At least one year of relevant professional work experience
English proficiency level A2 (Elementary Basic User) according to CEFR (Common European Framework of Reference for Languages) and fluency in written and spoken Vietnamese
Prior experience or training in project management is preferable.
Strong willingness and passion to help the vulnerable and disadvantaged in Vietnam.
Skills Required:
Excellent planning, time management and organizational skills; high level of integrity.
Comfortable using Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and email.
Ability to travel frequently to remote villages in varying weather conditions
Humble and mature team player; ability to be flexible and adapt.
Samaritan’s Purse in Vietnam is a Christian nonprofit organization. Candidates having working experience in a similar kind of organization will be an advantage.
Please note that Applications will be treated in confidence, only candidates invited to an interview will be contacted.
To respond to this opportunity, please send your
Cover letter, Samaritan’s Purse
Application form
(as attached) to
[email protected]
referencing “Vinh Long Project Administrator Opportunity” in the subject title.
Application Deadline:
June 30, 2026.

Job Title: Project Lead
Job Description:
will be fully responsible for the project implementation in Yen Bai Province. The primary goal is to empower women and youth through entrepreneurship and vocational training, specifically in the tourism and hospitality industry. This is a key leadership position, ensuring project targets are met on time, within budget, and with sustainable impact for the community.
A. Main tasks
The Project Manager will coordinate, lead, and oversee all project activities. This position requires strong team management skills, partnership building abilities, and high adaptability to local contexts, especially in rural and disadvantaged areas.
1. Project Management and Leadership
Lead and manage the project team, ensuring smooth implementation of all activities.
Assign tasks, monitor progress, and support the team in resolving emerging issues during implementation.
2. Training Design and Oversight
Design, develop and oversee tourism and hospitality training programs for local women and youth.
Ensure training content is aligned with labor market needs and the local community context.
3. Partnership Building and Maintenance
Build and maintain strong relationships with local stakeholders, including businesses, community groups, and government agencies.
Connect graduates with job and internship opportunities through the partner network.
4. Monitoring, Evaluation and Reporting
Monitor and evaluate project progress, ensuring goals and objectives are met on time and within approved budget.
Prepare regular and ad-hoc reports for donors, partners, and internal teams, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and timeliness.
Manage project budget, expenditures, and financial reporting in compliance with organizational and donor regulations.
5. Other Tasks
Perform other tasks as assigned by the Director, in compliance with REACH policies and regulations.
B. Expected Key Results
Successful implementation: Project is delivered on time, meeting or exceeding quantitative and qualitative targets.
Quality training: Tourism & hospitality training programs are well-designed, effectively delivered, and graduates meet labor market requirements.
Sustainable partnerships: Partner network is expanded and well-maintained, contributing to job and livelihood opportunities for graduates.
Timely, quality reporting: Progress, financial, and results reports are submitted fully, accurately, and on time as required by donors and the organization.
Effective budget management: Project is implemented within approved budget, ensuring transparency and financial compliance.
C. Skill and Qualification Requirements
Age: Male/Female under 40 years old.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in
Business Administration, Hospitality & Tourism, Social Work
, or a related field.
A Master’s degree is a strong advantage.
Experience: Minimum
5 years of experience
managing development projects, preferably those related to women and youth empowerment, entrepreneurship, and vocational training.
Experience working in rural, disadvantaged areas or with ethnic minority communities is an advantage.
Technical Knowledge: Strong understanding of the tourism and hospitality sector, especially in rural or community-based settings. Knowledge of entrepreneurship models and small business development for women and youth.
Leadership and Management Skills: Proven leadership skills with experience managing teams and budgets. Strong problem-solving, decision-making, and adaptability skills to handle on-the-ground challenges.
Communication and Partnership Skills: Excellent communication and relationship-building skills with diverse stakeholders (government, businesses, organizations, communities). Ability to work with diverse groups, especially local communities and women-led businesses.
Language: Basic English proficiency is required; fluency in English is a plus.
Commitment: Demonstrated commitment to REACH’s Vision and Mission.
D. Benefits:
Salary: Negotiable, based on competence and expreince
Working hours: Administrative hours, Monday to Friday.
Leave: 15 annual leave days/year + 12 days of support leave for employees with children under 36 months.
Working environment: Friendly, meaningful, and with high social impact.
Development opportunities: Access to training and professional development with local and international experts.

Job Title: Technical Advisor – Market-Based Programming
Job Description:
Under leadership and management of the Program Development Manager, The Technical Advisor – Market‑Based Programming (MBP) provides technical and strategic leadership to strengthen the design and application of inclusive, market‑based and livelihood approaches across Save the Children in Vietnam’s program.
The role supports the development of market‑based programming as a strategic and emerging pillar, contributing to child poverty reduction, livelihood resilience, and sustainability, aligned with the Country Strategic Plan, in the context of climate change, digital transformation, and evolving donor priorities.
The post holder will work with other Technical Advisors, other team members of the Program Development team, and Project Managers to integrate market-based approaches across Save the Children’s thematic sectors, including education, health, child protection, child rights governance, and climate resilience.
The Technical Advisor will play a critical role in driving resource mobilization by designing high-quality, competitive proposals for securing USD5 million annually from institutional, corporate, philanthropic, and innovative financing sources.
I. KEY AREAS OF ACCOUNTABILITY:
Strategic Leadership in Market-Based Programming
Lead the development and refinement of the market‑based programming technical approach, aligned with CSP priorities.
Translate child poverty and livelihoods strategy into practical, implementable MBP designs
Ensure integration of climate resilience, gender equality, inclusion, and safeguarding into MBP designs
Provide strategic advice on where MBP is appropriate and where it is not, based on evidence and feasibility
Position Save the Children as a thought leader in market-based programming for vulnerable children and youth within Vietnam and the region.
Program Design and Technical Excellence
Provide technical leadership in designing high-quality programs incorporating market-based solutions, including:
Sustainable and inclusive value chain development
Youth employment and entrepreneurship
Financial inclusion and access to services
Climate-smart livelihoods
Support technical advisors across sectors to embed market-based approaches into traditional programming (education, health, child protection, CRG, climate).
Develop tools, frameworks, and guidance for implementing market-based programming.
Ensure all programs are child-focused, inclusive, and aligned with safeguarding and equity principles.
Resource Mobilization and Proposal Development
Lead the development of high-quality concept notes and proposals on market-based programming and integrated approaches.
Work in close collaboration with the Program Development Manager to mobilize a minimum of USD 5 million annually through strategic fundraising efforts.
Identify and pursue funding opportunities from institutional donors, private sector, foundations, and innovative finance mechanisms.
Collaborate with Business Development, Finance, and Technical teams to ensure strong, competitive proposals.
Contribute to capture planning, pre-positioning, and donor engagement strategies.
Partnerships and Stakeholder Engagement
Build and maintain strategic partnerships with:
Government agencies
Private sector companies and industry associations
Financial institutions and impact investors
NGOs and development partners
Facilitate multi-stakeholder collaboration to scale market-based solutions.
Represent Save the Children in external forums, networks, and technical working groups.
Strengthen linkages with regional and global initiatives on market-based programming and innovative finance.
Innovation and Blended Finance
Lead the exploration and application of innovative financing models, including blended finance, results-based financing, and impact investment.
Identify opportunities to leverage private capital for social impact.
Integrate digital solutions and platforms into livelihood and economic programs.
Pilot and scale innovative approaches to enhance program sustainability and impact.
Capacity Building and Knowledge Management
Build capacity of Save the Children staff and partners on market-based programming approaches.
Provide coaching and mentoring to technical teams and project staff.
Capture and disseminate learning, best practices, and evidence.
Contribute to organizational knowledge and influence internal and external learning agendas.
II. BEHAVIOURS (Values in Practice
)
Accountability:
Holds self-accountable for making decisions, managing resources efficiently, achieving and role modelling Save the Children values
Holds the team and partners accountable to deliver on their responsibilities – giving them the freedom to deliver in the best way they see fit, providing the necessary development to improve performance and applying appropriate consequences when results are not achieved.
Ambition:
Sets ambitious and challenging goals for themselves and their team, takes responsibility for their own personal development and encourages their team to do the same
Widely shares their personal vision for Save the Children, engages and motivates others
Future orientated, thinks strategically and on a global scale.
Collaboration:
Builds and maintains effective relationships, with their team, colleagues, Members and external partners and supporters
Values diversity, sees it as a source of competitive strength
Approachable, good listener, easy to talk to.
Creativity:
Develops and encourages new and innovative solutions
Willing to take disciplined risks.
Integrity:
Honest, encourages openness and transparency
III. QUALIFICATIONS
Master’s degree or equivalent in Economics, Development Studies, Business Administration, International Development, or a related field
EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS:
Experience:
Minimum 5 years of experience in international development, with strong expertise in market-based programming, livelihoods, or economic development for vulnerable groups and/or difficult areas.
Proven experience in designing and implement

Monitor and work directly with partner to make sure that SCI finance and procurement policies and procedures are strictly followed. Report to Project Manager any suspected violation of SCI finance and procurement policies and procedure.

Job Title: Consultancy service provider(s) to support to develop report on situation of wildlife crime and the law enforcement response in Viet Nam in 2024-2025
Job Description:
Thông báo tìm đơn vị dịch vụ tư vấn:
Hỗ trợ quá trình xây dựng Báo cáo tình hình tội phạm và thực thi pháp luật về bảo vệ động vật hoang dã tại Việt Nam, giai đoạn 2024-2025
Service (to be performed):
Support to develop report on situation of wildlife crime and the law enforcement response in Viet Nam in 2024-2025
Dịch vụ
(
sẽ cung cấp
):
Hỗ trợ quá trình xây dựng Báo cáo tình hình tội phạm và thực thi pháp luật về bảo vệ động vật hoang dã tại Việt Nam, giai đoạn 2024-2025
Location of Service provided: Ha Noi
Địa điểm thực hiện: Hà Nội
Duration of Service’s performance: June – November 2026
Thời gian thực hiện dự kiến: Tháng 6-11/2026
I.
Background:
Since 2016, WCS Viet Nam has worked with the Criminal Statistics and Digital Transformation Bureau (CSB or Department 2) of Supreme People’s Procuracy (SPP) on collecting data and developing report on wildlife violations and crimes and the law enforcement of relevant agencies. After couple of years with four reports for 2013-2017, 2018-2019, 2020-2021, and 2023-2024 periods have been developed and published.
Implementing the cooperation plan between WCS and the SPP in 2025-2026, WCS Viet Nam has been coordinating with Department 2 to improve and integrate the automated data collection and statistical forms of wildlife case into the online data collection system. The online forms will be used to collect data from all 34 provincial and provide inputs to develop a report on situation of wildlife crime and the law enforcement response in Viet Nam during the 2024-2025 period. Comments and feedback on the data collection forms will also be collected to finetune the online data collection system.
To provide additional support to WCS Viet Nam and Department 2 of SPP, WCS Viet Nam is seeking a team of consultants to undertake specific tasks as detailed in the below sections.
I.
Bối cảnh:
Từ năm 2016, WCS Việt Nam đã phối hợp với Viện kiểm sát nhân dân (VKSND) tối cao, thông qua đầu mối là Cục Thống kê tội phạm và Chuyển đổi số (Cục 2), tiến hành thu thập số liệu và xây dựng báo cáo đánh giá tình hình vi phạm pháp luật về bảo vệ động vật hoang dã (ĐVHD) và hiệu quả thực thi pháp luật của các cơ quan liên quan tại Việt Nam. Cho đến nay, đã có bốn bản báo cáo theo bốn giai đoạn (2013-2017, 2018-2019, 2020-2021 và 2022-2023) được xây dựng và công bố.
Thực hiện kế hoạch hợp tác giữa WCS và VKSND tối cao năm 2025-2026, WCS Việt Nam đã phối hợp với Cục 2 chỉnh sửa và tích hợp mẫu biểu thu thập thông tin các vụ án về ĐVHD vào hệ thống trực tuyến. Mẫu biểu này sẽ được dùng để thu thập dữ liệu vi phạm về ĐVHD trên cả nước và phục vụ xây dựng Báo cáo tình hình tội phạm và thực thi pháp luật về bảo vệ ĐVHD tại Việt Nam, giai đoạn 2024-2025. Ý kiến ​​đóng góp và phản hồi về các biểu mẫu thu thập dữ liệu cũng sẽ được thu thập để hoàn thiện hệ thống thu thập dữ liệu trực tuyến
Nhằm cung cấp hỗ trợ cho WCS Việt Nam và Cục 2 VKSND tối cao, WCS Việt Nam tuyển một nhóm tư vấn để thực hiện các nhiệm vụ cụ thể được trình bày trong các phần dưới đây.
II.
Objectives:
Group of consultants will support WCS Viet Nam and Department 2 of SPP to carry out activities to achieve the following objectives:
To assess the situation of wildlife crime nationwide and the effectiveness of the criminal justice system in handling wildlife crime cases for the period of 2024-2025
Field test the online data collection and analysis forms and a User Guide with all 34 provincial procuracies nationwide and provide a summary of to-be-improved suggestions
II.
Mục tiêu:
Nhóm chuyên gia sẽ hỗ trợ WCS Việt Nam và Cục 2 VKSND tối cao thực hiện các hoạt động nhằm đạt được các mục tiêu sau:
Đánh giá được tình hình tội phạm về ĐVHD trên toàn quốc; cũng như hiệu quả của hệ thống tư pháp hình sự trong việc giải quyết các vụ án về ĐVHD giai đoạn 2024-2025.
Thử nghiệm các biểu mẫu thu thập và phân tích dữ liệu trực tuyến và hướng dẫn sử dụng với 34 Viện kiểm sát cấp tỉnh, thành phố trên toàn quốc, đồng thời tổng hợp các ý kiến phản hồi và đề xuất cải thiện
III.
Description of work:
Group of consultants will carry out the following activities:

Job Title: Research Assistant in Climate Change and Landscape Resilience
Job Description:
Overview
The primary purpose of this Research Assistant position is to support the National Project Coordinator in the effective coordination and timely implementation of project activities, with a particular focus on research, stakeholders’ facilitation and networking, and knowledge generation components. The position will also support communication with external partner. The Research Assistant will contribute, under the guidance of National Project Coordinator and Technical Project Leads, to the production of scientific and technical outputs as defined in the project documents, including literature reviews, data collection and analysis support, drafting technical notes and reports, and tracking deliverables required by the project coordinator. The position will also support coordination with local and national government agencies, particularly in relation to research activities, policy-relevant analysis, and validation of findings.
In addition, the role involves supporting the organization and facilitation of project workshops, trainings, and consultation meetings; assisting with fieldwork planning and implementation; and providing logistical and technical support for stakeholder engagement. When required, the Research Assistant may support external communications and represent the project in meetings or events, under the supervision of senior project staff.
The post holder will support the implementation of the following CIFOR-ICRAF joined projects:
Carbon for Good: Enabling a High-quality Forestry Carbon Market in Vietnam
UBS Optimus Foundation project Phase 1: Enabling a High Quality – High Integrity Blue Carbon Market in Vietnam and upcoming Phase 2: Building the Commercial and Investment Case for Blue Carbon in Vietnam
Other projects as tasked by the Viet Nam Country Coordinator and/ or CCE Theme Leader.
Tasks and Responsibilities Research (45%)
Support in designing methodology and conducting research activities (data collection and analysis) on the topics of carbon credit, climate change and landscape resilience, and other relevant domains.
Support and contribute to developing tools, manuals, and guidelines.
Support in managing databases on policy and regulations and other data and information gained from primary and secondary sources.
Support and lead in writing technical and policy briefs, peer-reviewed articles, book chapters, working papers, and other technical publications.
Support for designing research activities for, and co-supervise students and interns.
Support in preparing scientific presentations to and technical meetings with national and sub-national governments, partners, and donors.
Tasks and Responsibilities in Project Implementation (45%)
Support the Project Coordinator in developing reports and workplans as well as budget of relevant project(s) in collaboration with other team members and project partners.
Support the Project Coordinator in ensuring accountability processes to donor(s) by contributing to timely submissions of activities and reports on the ground.
Contribute to producing high-quality outputs and timely submissions of consultant and partner research outputs.
Support the preparations of reports and minutes-of-meetings for various meetings, seminars, and workshops with national and local governments and other partners.
Support the management of project logistics, documentation, and data, ensuring that research processes are efficient, which is a core component of capacity building.
Support the Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning Assessment process within CIFOR-ICRAF, and contribute to the process at the project level, in collaboration with donors and partners.
Capacity development (5%)
Support in coordinating and facilitating workshops and training initiatives to contribute to the overall improvement of institutional research capacity and strengthening the capacity of partners at national and sub-national levels.
Facilitate and join the fieldwork of students and interns, or project teams, as needed.
Other Responsibilities (5%)
Take reasonable care of own occupational health and safety and that of others as obliged by CIFOR-ICRAF’s Occupational Health and Safety Policy and cooperate with CIFOR-ICRAF in complying with requirements under any statutory health and safety provisions of relevant Country laws.
Participate in CIFOR-ICRAF Vietnam projects as required.
Other tasks as required.
Requirements
Education, knowledge, and experience:
Bachelor’s degree in agriculture, forestry, ecology, geography, environmental science and policy, environmental economics, or a related field.
Good knowledge of ecosystem services, sustainable landscapes, carbon markets, payment for forest ecosystem services (PFES), climate change issues and policy, participatory approach, benefit sharing mechanisms, Local Community’s rights and benefit sharing. Knowledge on mangrove ecosystems in Vietnam and globally is an advantage.
Good knowledge of Vietnam policy and regulation on agriculture, agroforestry, forestry, ecosystem services, and carbon market project.
Minimum 3 years’ of relevant professional experience as a researcher/research assistant position in reputable international or national organisations.
Experience in and passion for technical delivery. Familiarity with spatial analysis, remote sensing and data management will be an asset
Experience with project management, reporting, monitoring, and evaluation.
Experience working with multiple partners, including but not limited to national and sub-national government, academic partners, NGOs and local communities.
Experience with climate change mitigation in the agriculture, forestry, and other land use (AFOLU) sector, monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) systems, carbon project development and implementation for voluntary and/or compliance markets, and/or green financing will be an asset.
Personal Attributes and Competencies
Ability to work independently and in teamwork environment
Excellent interpersonal skills

With a sustainable history working in Vietnam, the current projects include health, education, relief, protection, water, sanitation, and hygiene projects in schools, and livelihood projects.
Samaritan’s Purse Vietnam’s projects are being implemented in Ha Noi, Lai Chau, Lao Cai, Da Nang, Ho Chi Minh and Vinh Long, where the majority of people are from ethnic minority groups, often the most vulnerable or marginalized sections of the population.

Reporting and Data Entry:

anagement:

Job Title: [Extension] Consultancy Service: Technical Support for Improving Brocade Products Based on Market Orientation for Producer Groups in Thanh Hoa Province
Job Description:
WORLD VISION INTERNATIONAL
IN VIETNAM
MICROFINANCE UNIT (MFU)
TERMS OF REFERENCES (TOR)
Consultancy Service: Technical Support for Improving Brocade Products Based on Market Orientation
for Producer Groups in Thanh Hoa Province
CLICK
HERE
FOR VIETNAMESE
I. BACKGROUND
:
The THRIVE Project is jointly implemented by the Microfinance Unit (MFU) and World Vision International in Vietnam, aiming to support vulnerable households in improving sustainable livelihoods, strengthening income security, and increasing market access.
Under the iMKA (Inclusive Market Knowledge and Access) component, the project prioritizes supporting producer groups with potential to participate in local value chains. Previous assessments conducted by MFU identified general production and business conditions of producer groups in the project areas. However, for brocade producer groups, further technical support is needed to improve product quality and market readiness.
Current challenges of brocade groups include:
Inconsistent finished product quality
Limited product diversity and design adaptation
Lack of basic quality control systems
Limited capacity for product improvement and market-oriented development
Therefore, the project seeks a qualified consultant/team to provide technical support for assessing existing brocade products and improving product quality based on market requirements, with the aim of strengthening future market linkage opportunities.
II. SCOPE OF WORK
The consultant/team will provide technical support to assess the market suitability of existing brocade products and support producer groups in improving product quality and product presentation.
Main tasks include:
1. Product Assessment
Assess selected brocade products of producer groups
Review product quality, finishing, consistency, design, and market suitability
Identify technical gaps and improvement needs
Recommend potential products for further market development
2. Development of Technical Support Program
Based on the assessment results, the consultant will develop a practical technical support and training program tailored to the needs of producer groups. Support may include:
Improving selected production techniques
Enhancing product finishing and consistency
Supporting product design adjustments for target markets
Standardizing selected production steps
3. Technical Support and Training Implementation
The consultant will organize technical support activities using appropriate methods (including online and offline), prioritizing practical coaching and on-site guidance. Activities may include:
Technical coaching sessions
Hands-on product improvement practice
Provision of technical guidance materials
Follow-up support during implementation
4. Final Assessment and Recommendations
At the end of the assignment, the consultant will submit a final report including:
Initial product assessment results
Technical support activities conducted
Improvements achieved in selected sample products
Remaining challenges and recommendations for future support
* IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE: 06/2026 – 09/2026
III. EXPECTED DELIVERABLES
Within the framework of this technical support package, the consultant/team is expected to achieve the following results:
1. Initial Market-Oriented Product Assessment
The consultant/team will assess selected existing brocade products of the producer groups based on target market requirements and provide an initial assessment report. The report may include:
Assessment of the finishing quality of existing products
Assessment of product consistency and uniformity
Assessment of the suitability of product designs for the target market orientation
Identification of technical issues related to product quality that require improvement
Identification of priority technical support needs of the producer groups
Recommendation of potential products for further improvement and future market linkage opportunities
The assessment report will serve as the basis for developing the technical support program for the next implementation phase.
2. Technical Support Program for Product Improvement Developed
The consultant/team will develop a technical support and training program based on the initial assessment results and the actual conditions of the producer groups. The support program should include:
Technical support topics relevant to product improvement needs
Appropriate implementation methods suitable for the local production context
Proposed implementation and training plan
Technical guidance materials for application by producer groups (if applicable)
3. Sample Products Improved Through Technical Support Activities
Following the implementation of technical support activities:
Selected sample products of the producer groups are improved in terms of product finishing and quality
Product consistency and uniformity are enhanced
Producer groups are able to apply selected technical improvements in their actual production process
Improved sample products are initially assessed for their suitability to target market requirements
4. Final Report on Training Results and Capacity Improvement
Upon completion of the assignment, the consultant/team will provide a final report summarizing implementation results, including:
Technical support activities conducted
Results achieved in improving sample products
Progress in the application of technical improvements by producer groups
Remaining challenges and constraints
Recommendations for further technical support and product development (if applicable)
IV. CONSULTANT REQUIREMENTS
1. Professional Experience and Expertise
The consultant/team should have:
Proven expertise in traditional handicraft product development, handmade textile production, or livelihood development based on handicraft production (preferably brocade weaving or similar products)
At least 3–5 years of experience in providing technical training, skills improvement, or production capacity development support for small-scale household producer groups

Job Title: Request for Expressions of Interest: Senior ESG Consultant for Sustainable Coffee Exchange (SCE) Project
Job Description:
Organisation
SNV Netherlands Development Organisation
Project
Sustainable Coffee Exchange (SCE)
Assignment
Senior ESG Consultant
Location
Remote – with frequent travel to Lam Dong and Son La
Duration
Part-time consultancy, 2026–2029 (output-based)
Reporting to/working with SNV focal point(s)
Project Manager, SCE
Starting date
Tentatively in August 2026 (upon contract signature)
Consultancy type
Individual National Consultant
Background
SNV is a global development partner rooted in the African and Asian countries where we operate. With 60 years of experience and a team of approximately 1,600 people, it is our mission to strengthen capacities and catalyse partnerships that transform the agri-food, energy, and water systems to enable sustainable and more equitable lives for all. These efforts are guided by cross-cutting themes such as climate adaptation and mitigation, gender equality and social inclusion (GEDSI), and strong institutions and effective governance.
In Viet Nam, SNV has been active since 1995, collaborating with communities, government agencies, and businesses across more than 50 provinces.
Background and objectives of assignment
SNV Viet Nam is implementing the Sustainable Coffee Exchange (SCE) project, co-funded by the European Union and JDE Peet’s. The project operates in Lam Dong and Son La provinces and targets the transformation of Viet Nam’s coffee sector toward ESG sustainability standards and EU market compliance, including the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR). The project directly engages 65 non-state actors (NSAs), facilitates 12 multi-stakeholder platforms, and aims for an improvement in ESG performance across target MSMEs and cooperatives.
The Senior ESG Consultant provides strategic leadership and technical oversight across three integrated areas: the design and roll-out of an ESG scorecard and tools; ongoing ESG adoption advisory services and ESG fundamentals training; and practical investor-readiness guidance for MSMEs and cooperatives. The Consultant also manages and mentors two provincial ESG Specialists (Lam Dong and Son La) to ensure consistent quality and delivery across both provinces.
Scope of work and key responsibilities
Strategic leadership
Provide overall strategic guidance on ESG integration across SCE project activities and connection to the ESG community in Viet Nam and the region.
Ensure project ESG strategies align with international standards including UN SDGs, IFC Performance Standards, OECD Guidelines, EUDR, CSRD, and other standards relevant to the size of businesses targeted by the program.
Oversee development and roll-out of the ESG scorecard and advisory services for MSMEs and cooperatives.
Management and coordination
Supervise and mentor two provincial ESG Specialists, ensuring consistent approaches and quality delivery across Lam Dong and Son La.
Coordinate with project partners — SNV, MSD, business associations, and CSOs — to align ESG technical support.
Facilitate cross-learning and knowledge exchange between the two provinces.
Introduce project to key working groups, associations, and gatherings of the ESG community in Viet Nam
Technical support
Co-develop a minimum viable ESG scorecard with business associations and cooperative alliances, contextualised to Viet Nam’s coffee sector with GEDSI and relevant EU export dimensions embedded; pilot with at least five MSMEs and cooperatives, then roll out to all target MSMEs and cooperatives with individual action plans.
Co-design ESG tools and templates for the SCE ESG Resource Hub, including a toolkit on the role of NSAs in advancing ESG in Viet Nam.
Provide one-on-one ESG adoption coaching and advisory services to target MSMEs and cooperatives via business associations and cooperative alliances throughout the project.
Develop practical investor-readiness guidance, including a pitch guide and ESG reporting checklist applicable fora range of investors; support planning and delivery of at least one investor matchmaking event.
Lead ESG scorecard implementation and support development of tailored action plans for MSMEs and cooperatives.
Provide technical input to Citizen-Generated Data initiatives and multi-stakeholder dialogues.
Capacity building and learning
Design and with support from ESG Advisors deliver ESG training modules for MSMEs, cooperatives, business associations, and CSOs.
Strengthen the capacity of NSAs to measure, report, and advocate for ESG adoption.

COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY AND INCLUSION
We are committed to ensuring diversity and gender equality within our organization.
Oxfam is a global movement of people who are fighting inequality to end poverty and injustice. Oxfam Confederation currently has 21 member organizations working in 79 countries. We share a vision of a just and sustainable world. A world where people and the planet are at the center of our economy. Where women and girls live free from violence and discrimination. Where the climate crisis is contained. And where governance systems are inclusive and allow for those in power to be held to account.
Oxfam in Vietnam believes that a reduction in poverty, injustice, and inequality will occur through the interaction between active citizens, accountable states and responsible private sector, and that it is fundamental to Vietnam’s development. Oxfam in Vietnam contributes to a shift from the current growth-based development model to a Human Economy that cares about People and the Planet.
All our work is led by our core values: Empowerment, Accountability, Inclusiveness, Courage, Solidarity and Equality.
JOB PURPOSE:
To provide technical leadership and delivery assurance for inclusive, gender responsive and market-relevant Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), under  Oxfam’s project “Strengthened and Inclusive Access to Training and Skills” (SIATS).
The role is primarily a technical specialist function.  Project or partner management responsibilities may be assigned where required, but the core accountability is to ensure that the project’s TVET approach remains technically sound, inclusive, evidence-based, market-responsive, and aligned with the approved theory of change, PMF, and implementation plan.
ROLE REPORTS TO

N/A

:  Lead technical quality assurance for the SIATS TVET approach, ensuring that project strategies, methodologies, tools, and partner workplans are aligned with inclusive, gender-responsive, and market-relevant TVET standards, the project theory of change, PMF, and approved implementation plan.
2) TVET system strengthening
: Provide technical guidance to Oxfam, and its partners on strengthening inclusive TVET systems, including gender analysis, accessibility, trainee feedback, curriculum adaptation, trainer capacity, institutional learning and service improvement.
3) Demand-Supply linkage and labour market alignment:
Lead technical integration of demand-supply linkages by connecting the needs, aspirations and feedback of vulnerable and marginalised women with PVET training provision, employer expectations, labour-market trends and private-sector demand.
4) Evidence-based adaptive programming
: Support the effective design, rollout and use of M-score as a TVET system-strengthening mechanism.  Ensure that M-score and related feedback systems generate usable data on trainee experience, training relevance, accessibility barriers, job trends, and employment outcomes, and that the evidence is used by partners, employers, and Oxfam for adaptive programming and service improvement.
5) Partner capacity strengthening

6) Learning, knowledge and insutitionalisation
Work with the MEAL, Knowledge Management & Communications Unit, document good practices, technical lessons, and evidence from implementation.  Contribute to inclusive and gender-responsive TVET practices through guidance and learning products and engagement with relevant TVET reform actors.
Technical inputs for donor engagement and resource mobilisastion: Provide technical inputs for Government of Canada (GAC) reporting, adaptive management discussions, donor updates, proposal development and resource mobilisastion related to TVET, skills development, women’s economic empowerment, and inclusive labour-market systems.
Representation and external engagement: Represent Oxfam as a technical focal point on inclusive, gender responsive, and market-relevant vocational training and education TVET and advocacy to institutions, government, development partners, the private sector, Oxfam networks, and relevant national or sectoral platforms.
7) Other
Ensure the implementation of Oxfam’s operational policies, procedures and guidelines (especially gender equity, child protection) in all aspects of Oxfam’s work.
Link country strategy to global initiatives, support, and contribute to global advocacy initiatives.

1. Introduction:
Plan International is an independent development and humanitarian organisation that advances children’s rights and equality for girls. We believe in the power and potential of every child but know this is often suppressed by poverty, violence, exclusion, and discrimination. And it is girls who are most affected. Working together with children, young people, supporters, and partners, we strive for a just world, tackling the root causes of the challenges girls and vulnerable children face. We support children’s rights from birth until they reach adulthood, and we enable children to prepare for and respond to crises and adversity. We drive changes in practice and policy at local, national, and global levels using our reach, experience, and knowledge. For over 85 years, we have rallied other determined optimists to transform the lives of all children in more than 80 countries. We won’t stop until we are all equal.
Plan International has been working in northern and central Vietnam since 1993. Our work supports marginalised children and youth, especially adolescent girls, to start life and grow up physically and mentally healthy and as adolescents so they are leading the way to shape their own future. We believe girls have the power to change the world. Our ambition is to work alongside them and together take action so that 2 million girls in Vietnam can learn, lead, decide and thrive by 2027.

Job Title: Deputy Team Leader
Job Description:
to support the implementation of the exciting flagship TRVC that runs until 2027. We are looking for an individual with
excellent project management and team leadership experience, and a keen interest in carbon markets, working with private sector, and a commitment to promoting gender and social inclusion.
About SNV Vietnam
SNV is a mission-driven global development partner working in more than 20 countries across Africa and Asia. Building on 60 years of experience and grounded in the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, we work on the core themes of gender equality and social inclusion, climate adaptation and mitigation, and strong institutions and effective governance. Together with our team of over 1,600 people world-wide, our mission is to strengthen capacities and catalyse partnerships that transform the agri-food, energy, and water systems, which enable sustainable and more equitable lives for all.
Our core values people-centeredness and respect, equity and equality, and diversity and inclusion, are fundamental to who we are, and what we do. This is reflected in our vision and mission, and strategy, which sets out our aspirations and commitments as our compass towards 2030.
SNV has been working in Vietnam since 1995, and currently has over 50 national and international staff. SNV Vietnam’s team works hand-in-hand with communities, government agencies, and businesses in more than 30 provinces, including the remotest and poorest areas of the country. We operate from a country office in Hanoi as well as project offices in Ho Chi Minh City, Tra Vinh, and Buon Ma Thuot. Our work focuses on three sectors – Agri-Food, Water and Energy.
Program Summary
In the Mekong Delta of Vietnam, SNV Vietnam is implementing the project
“Transforming the Rice Value Chain for Climate Resilient and Sustainable Development“ (TRVC)
in An Giang, Dong Thap, and Kien Giang provinces. This exciting and innovative project aims to use a results-based financing mechanism to incentivize private enterprises in the rice value chain (RVC) to invest in scaling sustainable rice production among smallholder farmers (SHFs) and marketing domestically and internationally in the most rice intensive production provinces over five years (2022 2027). It is built on three core strategic objectives, namely
improving climate resilience,
optimizing rice value chain linkages, and
developing carbon markets.
Job Description
The Deputy Team Leader (DTL) supports the Team Leader in the overall management, coordination, and delivery of the TRVC Project, ensuring effective implementation across technical, operational, and cross-functional workstreams. The DTL serves as a central coordination point between project leadership, component leads, consultants, and provincial partners to ensure timely, high-quality, and compliant execution of project activities.
Key Responsibilities
1.Support the Team Leader with day-to-day Project Management and Implementation Activities
Support the Team Leader in managing daily project operations and implementation activities across all project components.
Coordinate with government counterparts, provincial authorities, consultants, partners, and relevant stakeholders to deliver project workplans and milestones.
Plan, assign, and monitor deliverables and activities, ensuring progress, quality, and timely completion within approved budgets.
Supervise and develop annual work planning, budgeting, reporting, learning reviews, and project closure processes.
Identify implementation risks, propose mitigation actions, and maintain oversight of the project risk register.
Act on behalf of the Team Leader when delegated.
2. Project Technical Lead and Quality Assurance
Lead in designing and implementing mini-Circular Economy pilots (i.e. rice straw, stubble and husk) within the project sites in An Giang and Dong Thap provinces. That include conducting technical studies/reviews of all contemporary best practices/modalities; writing baseline report and proposal for the most feasible and optimal mini-pilots; developing partnership for co-implementing, securing formal agreement and approval within project and local authorities as appropriate; and ensure the effective implementation of mini circular economic pilots and related activities under assigned portfolios.
Provide direct oversight of Operations, GEDSI and Safeguards component and assigned technical workstreams, including Knowledge Management, and Carbon Registration and Certification Oversee the quality, relevance, and timeliness of technical deliverables, reports, and knowledge products.
Ensure effective integration of GEDSI, safeguarding, PMEL, and operational requirements across project activities.
Support adaptive management by tracking progress, identifying bottlenecks, and recommending evidence-based improvements.
Ensure alignment of project approaches with national priorities and donor expectations.
3. People Management & Team Leadership
Supervise the Senior Project Coordinator, GEDSI Advisor and support alignment across project teams and consultants.
Guide, coach, and motivate team members to achieve high performance and collaborative working relationships.
Promote clear communication, accountability, and teamwork across central and site-level teams.
Support recruitment, onboarding, and performance management of project personnel as required.
Advise the Team Leader on staffing, resourcing, and team capacity needs.
4. Contract Management & Project Financial Oversight
Identify, develop ToRs and collaborate with departments for consultant recruitment and management.
Ensure sound financial management and compliance with donor and SNV policies and procedures.
Supports the Team Leader in budgeting and forecasting, and in risk management.
5. Analysis & Reporting
Provides technical and management guidance for project reporting; reviews and validates key messages, progress analysis, risks, corrective actions, and report quality before submission to the Team Leader.

Job Title: Consultancy to Provide Rehabilitation Services for People with Disabilities
Job Description:
CONSULTANCY SERVICES
Provision of Rehabilitation Services for People with Disabilities
1. BACKGROUND
The International Center (formerly known as Vietnam Veterans of America Foundation -VVAF) is a humanitarian organization that has addressed the consequences of war through advocacy and service programs in Vietnam for the last three decades. As a co-founder of the International Campaign to Ban Landmines, IC-VVAF was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1997. The International Center (IC) now focuses on disability support, landmine removal, and mental health programs.
Under the financial support of the United States Government for the Program of Enhancing the Quality of Life of Persons with Disabilities in heavily sprayed with Agent Orange provinces of Gia Lai and Quang Ngai (Inclusion 2b), as a sub-implementing partner,  IC is responsible for implementing the Objective One of the project which is to expand the rehabilitation services to contribute to the Goal that to improve the quality of life of persons with disabilities in provinces heavily sprayed with Agent Orange, namely Gia Lai and Quang Ngai.
Three expected results include:
Ensured accessibility and quality of multi-disciplinary rehabilitation services
Improved rehabilitation system for the provinces
Improved Governance of the rehabilitation service system
To achieve the abovementioned objectives, a wide range of activities will be implemented in the two project provinces and at the central level. Rehabilitation service provision follows the relevant steps: need assessment and prescription, service delivery, and outcome measurement. These steps are required to ensure that people with disabilities receive appropriate, high-quality, holistic rehabilitation services.
To supplement the effort of the Government to provide rehabilitation services to persons with disabilities in the provinces, IC’s project plans on identifying private service providers that can support and deliver comprehensive rehab services to persons with disabilities including physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy, and non-construction-based home modifications to persons with disabilities, leading to improve functions of daily living activities for persons with disabilities in Gia Lai and Quang Ngai provinces.
IC is seeking individuals/firms to join our dynamic team in providing multidisciplinary rehabilitation services in the community. Selected providers will have the chance to participate in the training on multidisciplinary rehabilitation service provision organized by IC prior to the delivery of the rehab service, as part of the capacity building for the local private service providers in the areas of multidisciplinary rehab services.
2. DUTY STATION
Duty Station: Gia Lai and Quang Ngai provinces.
Duration of Assignment: from 20 June to December 2026.
3. SCOPE OF WORK AND DELIVERABLES
The qualified individuals/ firms will carry out the following services:
Scope of work:
Collaborate and coordinate with local partners and IC to plan, prepare, and implement project activities in the fields.
Provide multidisciplinary rehabilitation services for persons with disabilities at the selected project sites in Gia Lai and Quang Ngai provinces, including, but not limited to, needs assessment, development of individual intervention plans, provision of intervention services in accordance with the plans, and outcome measurement for people with disabilities in Gia Lai and Quang Ngai provinces.
Provide services to non-construction home modification for persons with disabilities based on the need assessments;
Comply with the project’s procedures and instructions for providing rehabilitation services for people with disabilities in the assigned area.
Deliverables:
List of persons with disabilities who achieved/not achieved rehabilitation goals;
The list of persons with disabilities improved their activities of daily living, as assessed, and their prescriptions and initial goal settings.
Medical records, including detailed information on interventions, processes, and results, are maintained for each person.
Device handover minutes collected and given to the commune health stations managing persons with disabilities;
Photos and video from the last session were taken, documenting the improvement following the intervention.
A report summarizing the results of the intervention, pros and cons, and suggestions and recommendations, if needed (with the firm).
4. SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS
Qualifications and Experience Required:
For the firm: experience in providing multi-disciplinary rehabilitation services to persons with disabilities in the localities. Working with US-funded projects is an advantage.
For individuals:
A degree in Rehabilitation is required.
At least 5 years of experience in Rehabilitation service provision and at least 2 years of experience in applying FIM in need assessment for rehabilitation services
Minimum two years of relevant experience for persons with disabilities
Experience in working with diverse stakeholders is an asset.
Competencies:
Teamwork, professionalism, flexibility, and an understanding of working with people from diverse backgrounds.
Having dedication and ability to learn, to work independently and in groups, high sense of responsibility.
Willing to accept high frequency of business travel in project provinces in Vietnam.
Understand the local context and partnership.
5. TRAVEL AND FINANCES
Travel costs will be provided or reimbursed to the consultant, and per diem allowances will be provided or reimbursed for days traveled.
6. SUPERVISION AND PERFORMANCE EVALUATION
The consultants will work under the direct supervision and evaluation of Program Directors and Project Officers.
7. EVALUATION CRITERIAS
Relevant qualifications and experience
Technical proposal: Appropriate and adequate to requirements
Financial proposal: reasonable and relevant to expertise.
Combined technical and financial scores: Competitive
8. APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS:
Individuals

Job Title: Part-time Technical Consultant
Job Description:
VACANCY ANNOUNCEMENT
CLINTON HEALTH ACCESS INITIATIVE, INC. (CHAI)
The Clinton Health Access Initiative, Inc. (CHAI) is a global health organization committed to saving lives and reducing the burden of disease in low – and – middle – income countries. We work at the invitation of governments and the private sector to support them in creating and sustaining high-quality health systems. In Vietnam, CHAI has partnered with the Ministry of Health and national programs since 2006 across HIV, tuberculosis, malaria, immunization, digital health, and more recently, non-communicable diseases (NCDs).
Under the RESist-NCD project, CHAI Vietnam is working with the Vietnam Administration of Disease Prevention (VADP), local authorities, and technical partners to support strengthening NCD screening, early detection, and management activities at the primary health care level.
VADP is currently leading the review, update, and finalization of technical guidelines related to NCD screening, early detection, management, and prevention at the community level. CHAI, together with WHO and relevant technical partners, is providing technical support for this process.
To support the above activities, CHAI plans to recruit a part-time technical consultant to work with VADP and technical expert groups in reviewing documents, consolidating technical feedback, and editing and finalizing materials for the consultation and approval process. Please see the

Job Description:
The Mekong River Commission (MRC) is the only inter-governmental agency that works directly with the governments of Cambodia, Lao PDR, Thailand, and Vietnam on joint management of shared water resources and sustainable development of the Mekong River.
As a regional facilitating and advisory body governed by water and environment ministers of the four countries, the MRC aims to ensure that the Mekong water is managed and developed in the most efficient and sustainable manner—that mutually benefits all Member Countries and minimises harmful effects on people and the environment in the Lower Mekong River Basin.
As the MRC Secretariat works extensively with a diverse range of documents, including reports, briefs, and handbooks, the Secretariat in Vientiane, Lao PDR, is seeking a qualified candidate for the position of:

A minimum of 10 years of professional experience in project monitoring and evaluation, preferably in regional, environmental, fisheries, biodiversity, or natural resources management project;
Demonstrated experience in results-based management (RBM), monitoring frameworks, indicator development, data management, and donor reporting systems;

Job Title: Senior Water Governance Specialist
Job Location: Ho Chi Minh (preferred), Hanoi, Bangkok, Vientiane and Phnom Penh (also considered)
Job Description:
Who we are
The Alluvium Group
At Alluvium Group, we are dedicated professionals making a lasting impact for society, nature and the environment. Our multidisciplinary and technical expertise combined with an agile and innovative mindset set us apart in the industry. With a strong commitment to employee ownership, we foster a sense of belonging and shared success.
We offer a flexible working environment, profit sharing, professional memberships, ongoing training and development as well as international opportunities. Our internal research program reinvests 10% of profits into pioneering projects, promoting continuous growth and innovation. Every employee helps shape our direction and elevate industry standards.
We are driven by change, guided by science, and sustained by impact.
The Alluvium Group in the Mekong
The countries of the Lower Mekong Basin (LMB) are facing huge challenges in adapting their populations and economies to a rapidly changing climate. Alluvium takes a science based, ecosystem-focussed and collaborative approach to working with government, private sector and communities to address these challenges. Through this approach we are helping to build a greener and more resilient future for the region.
Alluvium’s work in the LMB is organised across three main programs: integrated water, climate resilience and energy transition. Within these programs, Alluvium is implementing projects across the Mekong region on a range of topics such as water scarcity, climate adaptation, impacts of hydropower development, climate resilient water management, naturebased solutions, river basin planning, water governance and water resources modelling.
We have grown across the region with team members now based in Ho Chi Minh City, Can Tho, Vientiane, Bangkok and Phnom Penh.
But there’s more work to do. We need your help in growing a thriving business providing trusted and innovative consulting services at the intersection of people and nature.
About this role
We are looking for a Senior Water Governance specialist to lead our work in the Lower Mekong Basin.
The role will lead water policy and strategy engagement with Government and private sector clients, deliver technical advice, and oversee project delivery of our water management-related projects across the Lower Mekong Basin and more broadly across Southeast Asia.
We are looking for someone who:
Has more than 10 years’ experience in water governance and / or associated disciplines related to water (river basin management / water resource management / climate change / nature based solutions / environment) in at least one of the following countries: Vietnam, Cambodia, Lao PDR, Thailand.
Has experience developing and implementing water management policies and/or strategies with Governments and private clients.
Has a strong network of peers in the water management sector across Mekong countries.
Has a strong technical grounding in relevant standards & software packages.
Shares our themes of culture, impact and value; and will reflect this in decision making and project delivery.
Ho Chi Minh City is preferred location
(cities such as Hanoi, Bangkok, Vientiane and Phnom Penh will be considered for excellent candidates).
What you will do
Provide technical input into Alluvium’s international projects with a focus on water resources governance in the countries of the lower Mekong.
Provide science-based input into a variety of projects spanning governance, policy, strategy, planning, capacity building, quantitative and qualitative analysis, design and data generation.
Lead proposal development with a focus on water resource governance in the countries of the lower Mekong.
Project Management for Alluvium’s international projects with a focus on water resources management in the countries of the lower Mekong.
Participate in business development activities with a focus on water resources management in the lower Mekong countries.
Develop and/or maintain relationships with key potential and existing clients and partners.
Support the Alluvium Group’s Research, Knowledge and Insights work focussed on the countries of the lower Mekong.
Work with the Regional Manager and Technical lead to build a practice and portfolio of work in a water management niche of our business relevant to your skill set.
Skills and Experience
Essential
PhD or Master’s Degree in relevant field
Minimum 10 years’ of relevant professional experience in one of the following: hydrology, climate change (water focus), river basin planning and governance or environmental engineering.
Demonstrated experience advising government counterparts on water policy, river basin planning, or transboundary water governance in at least one LMB country.
Track record of translating technical evidence into policy reform, regulatory instruments, or institutional strengthening.
Proven ability to lead proposals and convert relationships into a pipeline of work; including bid strategy, partner negotiation, and technical writing under tight timelines.
High-quality written and spoken English suitable for ministerial briefings, donor reports, and board-level audiences, with confidence representing the firm at regional fora and to mixed government, donor, private sector, and community audiences.
Fluency in one of the following languages: Vietnamese, Lao, Khmer, or Thai.
Established relationships with water sector decision-makers in at least one LMB country, including line ministries, basin organisations, or development partners.
Desirable
Ability to read the political economy of water reform in the Mekong
Experience shaping multi-year programs including setting direction, sequencing reform, and managing risk across political cycles.
Experience leading proposals for DFAT, ADB, World Bank, GIZ, or equivalent; including bid strategy, partner negotiation, and technical writing
Experience leading   complex, multi-country projects with budgets above AUD 1M,
Active networks across the Mekong water sector (e.g. university

Job Title: Request for Expressions of Interest from Coaches, Sector Advisors, and Business Consultants
Job Description:
About SNV
SNV is a global development partner rooted in the African and Asian countries where we operate. With 60 years of experience and a team of approximately 1,600 people, it is our mission to strengthen capacities and catalyse partnerships that transform the agri-food, energy, and water systems to enable sustainable and more equitable lives for all. These efforts are guided by cross-cutting themes such as climate adaptation and mitigation, gender equality and social inclusion, and strong institutions and effective governance.
In Vietnam, SNV has been active since 1995, collaborating with communities, government agencies, and businesses across more than 50 provinces.
Background and objectives of assignment
Under the Green Innovation Grants Initiative (GIGI) part of the Shifting Investment Flows Towards Green Transformation (SHIFT) project, SNV Netherlands Development Organisation supports innovative small and medium-sized (SMEs) developing and deploying innovative solutions that contribute to the energy transition and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reduction in Vietnam. Supported SMEs operate across a range of sectors, including renewable energy, energy efficiency, bioenergy, low-emission transport, digitalisation, and other clean energy or climate solutions.
To complement grant funding and strengthen implementation capacity, SNV is establishing a roster of pre-qualified experts who can provide targeted coaching, sector advisory services, and business consulting support based on the specific needs of supported SMEs and projects. Assignments may include both short-term technical assistance, strategic business advisory services, and ongoing coaching engagements focused on areas such as technical implementation, business growth, operational strengthening, investment readiness, and leadership support.
Level of effort
Participating SMEs may receive up to 15 hours of sector advisory service, business consulting support, and coaching over a 12-month period, depending on identified needs, programme priorities, and expert availability.
Assignments may be delivered virtually and/or in-person and may vary in scope and duration depending on programme requirements. All assignments will happen between July 2026 and December 2027.
Inclusion in the roster does not guarantee a minimum level of engagement or assignment.
Areas of Expertise
We are seeking experts with experience in one or more of the areas listed below:
Sector Advisors
Sector advisors provide sectoral and technical, guidance to support technology validation, deployment readiness, commercialisation, and scale-up in the clean energy and climate sector. Indicative areas of expertise include:
Renewable energy solutions (e.g. solar, wind, distributed energy systems, energy storage, etc.)
Energy efficiency and energy management
Bioenergy and biofuels
Low-emission transport and e-mobility solutions
Digitalisation and climate technology solutions
Greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions measurement and reporting
Environmental and social risk management
Energy and climate policy and regulatory advisory
Carbon markets and climate finance mechanisms
Business Consultants
Business consultants provide targeted, specialised business support requiring specific expertise and deliverable-oriented assignments. Indicative areas of expertise include:
Business model development and commercialisation
Financial planning and investment readiness
Market assessment and go-to-market strategy
Operations and growth strategy
Sales strategy (pricing, channels, partnerships)
Commercial contracting and deal structuring
Preparation of investment-grade materials (pitch deck, financial model, ESG plan, etc.)
Investor communication and pitch delivery
ESG/GESI integration into business plan and reporting
Market research and competitive analysis
Coaches
Coaches support founders and leadership teams in strengthening leadership, communication, strategic decision-making, and organisational effectiveness. Indicative areas of expertise include:
Leadership and management coaching for founders and senior management
Founder/Leadership effectiveness and organisational development
Strategic prioritisation and decision-making
Team management and communication
Founder/leadership resilience and adaptive leadership
Other experience and qualifications
Educational background:
Bachelor’s degree or higher in relevant technical, business, financial, legal, environmental, or related fields
Relevant professional certifications or coaching certifications are an advantage
Language requirements:
Professional working proficiency in English
Fluency in Vietnamese is highly desirable
Professional Experience:
Minimum 5 years of relevant professional experience
Demonstrated experience working with SMEs, startups, or growth-stage
Experience providing coaching, advisory, or technical assistance services
Familiarity with the Vietnam and/or Southeast Asia / Asia Pacific (APAC) market is required
Others:
Nationals of Vietnam belonging to historically underrepresented or disadvantaged groups (women, people with disabilities, people from ethnic minority groups, and other underrepresented communities) are strongly encouraged to apply.

individual consultants
are requested to submit the completed and signed Letter of Interest, available for download at

.
